example: One wheel level holds more targets than fit comfortably on a single rim; the surplus flows onto a second concentric ring instead of forcing the user into a group. Drag the 'targets' slider to watch the second ring appear.

variants.focus: Split balanced: past the single-ring threshold all tiles split evenly across two equal-size rings, each with its own even spacing. Roomiest tiles, largest window growth (~600px).
Overflow band: the inner ring stays the familiar wheel up to a cap; only the surplus lands on a new outer band, staggered into the gaps. The second ring appears only when needed.
Petals: tiles alternate between rings, the outer row offset into the gaps and slightly smaller. The most compact layout — smallest window growth, but a smaller drop target on the outer row.
Concentric columns: tiles form radial pairs on shared spokes; hover lights the whole column (both tiles and the spoke). Two distinct rim bands, structured grid feel.
